basic_details.job_title: Exim Bank Management Trainee Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Export-Import Bank of India (Exim Bank), Mumbai
basic_details.post_name: Management Trainee (MT) - Human Resource Management & Administration
basic_details.short_information: Exim Bank is recruiting 05 Management Trainee posts with MBA/PGDBA/PGDBM/MMS qualification, last date 14 June 2026.

important_dates.admit_card_date: To be notified on the Bank's website
important_dates.interview_date: To be notified after Written Test result

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application Fees / Intimation Charges through online mode only from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. Bank transaction charges for online payment will be borne by the candidate. The registration process is complete only when fee is deposited through online mode on or before the last date. Fees once paid will not be refunded on any account.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 21 years and maximum of 28 years as on 30-04-2026. Maximum age indicated is for General (Unreserved) candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen candidates.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates having completed graduation and postgraduation through correspondence / part-time / open university / open learning / external / distance learning are NOT eligible. All qualifications must be from a University/Institution recognised/approved by Government / AICTE / UGC. | Note: Candidates who have appeared in their final qualifying examinations and are awaiting results are eligible to appear in the Written Examination. However, the offer to join shall be subject to production of necessary documents evidencing successful completion of requisite qualification by 01-06-2026 or the joining date prescribed by the Bank (whichever is later). Candidates from universities/institutes awarding CGPA/GPA must furnish a letter from the Head of Institute/Controller of Examination indicating the conversion formula from grades to percentage, if equivalent percentage is not mentioned in their marksheet.

selection_process: Part I Subject Matter Knowledge - HR / Administration (Compulsory Question) 40 02 hours 30 minutes
Part II Attempt any 6 out of 8 Questions (HR / Administration) 60

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for Exim Bank MT Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very intense given that only 05 vacancies are on offer across two disciplines from a large pool of MBA/PGDBM graduates across India. The Bank being a premier financial institution under the Government of India makes this an exceptionally sought-after position among finance, HR, and administration postgraduates. The subjective Written Test format — unlike the MCQ-based bank exams — demands strong subject knowledge, analytical writing ability, and command over HR / Administration concepts rather than just speed and aptitude. | The low vacancy count means even marginal differences in written test scores will determine shortlisting for the interview round. Candidates with domain depth in HRM/Personnel Management (for MT-HRM) or Hotel/Facilities/Civil background with MBA (for MT-Administration) are better positioned. The Personal Interview carrying 30% of the final weightage means soft skills, subject clarity, and awareness of Exim Bank's functions and India's international trade ecosystem will be critical differentiators at the final stage.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026 through the link available at https://www.eximbankindia.in/careers. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date. Before applying, candidates must scan and keep ready all required documents as per specifications mentioned in the notification.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:
Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB-50 KB; JPEG)
Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB-20 KB; JPEG) — not in CAPITAL LETTERS
important_instructions: The application link will be live from 15 May 2026; no application in any other format or mode will be considered. The registration process is complete only when fee is deposited through online mode on or before the last date; incomplete applications or applications without fee (except exempt categories) are liable to be rejected. Candidates must ensure they fulfil all eligibility criteria before paying fee/submitting application; fees once paid will not be refunded even if the applicant is found ineligible. Management Trainees will be absorbed as Deputy Managers (JM-I) after successful completion of one year of training, subject to satisfactory performance. At the time of joining, selected candidates must execute a personal bond of Rs. 3 lakh to serve as Management Trainee for 1 year and minimum 5 years as an employee of the Bank after absorption. Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in India; candidates willing to serve anywhere in India only should apply. They may also be transferred to offices outside India with their consent. The Bank will analyse responses of candidates to detect similarity patterns; if scores are found not genuine/valid, the Bank reserves the right to cancel the candidature. Final merit list will be based on Written Examination (70% weightage) and Personal Interview (30% weightage). There will be no sectional cut-off in the Written Test; shortlisting for interview will be based on overall performance in the Written Test. A pre-recruitment training will be organised for candidates from reserved categories; details will be intimated to candidates who opt for it in the online application form. PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility must indicate the same in the online application form at the time of applying; subsequent requests may not be entertained. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at their own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination will be allowed for eligible PwBD candidates using a scribe. Mobile phones or similar electronic communication devices are strictly prohibited inside the examination/interview hall. No request for change of examination centre/venue/date/session will be entertained. Candidates must carry the call letter and original photo identity proof to both the Written Test and Personal Interview without fail; absence of either will result in denial of entry. Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification. The Bank reserves the right to cancel the recruitment process entirely at any stage without assigning any reason. All legal proceedings in respect of any claim/dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be instituted only in Mumbai; courts/tribunals/forums at Mumbai shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.
